notes: Benchmarks 15 open LLMs on 82 text-to-SQL tasks via execution-grounded labeling, then systematically varies unanimous LLM-jury committee size/composition and measures operating characteristics (TPR/FPR/Youden J). The systematic sweep over committee size and self-judging inclusion is experiment-like manipulation; human-authored ground-truth queries are a reference baseline, not human-subjects data.

Builds the SLEAN framework and evaluates it on a curated set of 15 autogenerated buggy codebases (69 AI fix propositions), scored via systematic content analysis and manual fix verification. Bug reports simulate a casual 'vibe coder' but are author-authored, not real participants, so no human subjects.

Qualitative thematic analysis of 57 curated YouTube videos (grey literature) following Garousi et al.'s multivocal literature review guidelines, including quality scoring and cross-validation. Analyzes existing public content rather than collecting data from human participants, so human_subjects is false. The educational-implications sections are conceptual discussion built on these findings.
